zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Murray於1219年之前與Alan Wyntoun在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Murray于1219年之前与Alan Wyntoun在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Murray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Moray in northeastern Scotland, which derives from Celtic mori- ‘sea’ + treb- ‘settlement’. The founder of the Scottish house of Murray was a Fleming named Freskin who was granted Strathbrock in West Lothian and Duffus in Moray by David I. The family took its name from the region in the late 12th century.
Irish and Scottish: shortened form of   McMurray  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muireadhaigh ‘descendant of Muireadhach’ a personal name meaning ‘mariner’. Occasionally it may be a shortened form of McMurray .

variants.name: Murry
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Murrah
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Murray  .

variants.name: Murie
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Murie in Errol (Perthshire).
Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Merhi
meanings: Arabic (mainly Lebanon and Syria): from a variant of the personal name Mirhi of unknown meaning. Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians. Compare Murray 5.

variants.name: McMurray
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muireadhaigh or Mac Muireadhaich, a patronymic from the personal name Muireadhach ‘seafarer’ (see Murdoch ). Compare McCurry 1.

variants.name: Murra
meanings: Italian (Sicily): topographic name from Sicilian murra ‘large rock, mass of rocks’ or a habitational name from a place called with this word.
Albanian: nickname from murrë (definite form murra) ‘dark gray cow’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Murray married Alan Wyntoun before 1219, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Murray a épousé Alan Wyntoun avant 1219, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Murray hat vor 1219 in Europa Alan Wyntoun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Murray вышла замуж за человека по имени Alan Wyntoun перед 1219 г. в местности Европа. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
